name: Transposon and Somatic Mutation Control Coverage Assessment for June 6, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Coverage assessment created during ordinary surveillance after the published June 2 bootstrap baseline.

summary: Coverage is enough to support the current low-confidence human_biomarker_signal public claim, but the source map is thin and needs repair. The public baseline is anchored by ART-AD 3TC open-label human biomarker evidence, LINE1/lamivudine mouse evidence, SIRT6 LINE1 mechanism, and CANTOS/TET2 CHIP inflammatory-outcome analyses. The surveillance pass found no post-publication result requiring immediate public update, but it surfaced missing pre-baseline and active-program context: FTC/TAF biological-age biomarker data in healthy adults, LINE-AD and SANTA emtricitabine or FTC/TAF registries, the ARPA-H/TPN-101 healthy-aging program, a TET2 CHIP canakinumab trial-watch record, and a same-window DREAM somatic-mutation mechanism paper. Because these are source-completeness and taxonomy-boundary issues, a bounded coverage-repair pass should review them before ordinary surveillance resumes.

evidence_categories.rationale: The public support map includes ART-AD, a 12-participant open-label 3TC study in early Alzheimer disease, as a low-confidence biomarker foothold. It does not yet represent pre-baseline FTC/TAF biological-age biomarker evidence in healthy adults or controlled emtricitabine or FTC/TAF Alzheimer trial-watch records. No curated source shows replicated controlled functional aging benefit, direct LINE1/retrotransposon target engagement in aging-relevant human tissue, or direct somatic mutation or clone-size control.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Review PMID 41929344, NCT04500847, NCT07210528, ARPA-H award 4201, Brown/University of Rochester TPN-101 program details, and Transposon Therapeutics program materials during coverage repair.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline covers LINE1/lamivudine aged-mouse inflammatory activation, old-mouse 3TC cognition/neuroinflammation, and lamivudine tauopathy mouse disease-model evidence. It does not yet review PMID 42230995, which reports lower mouse brain mutation accumulation after DREAM knockout but is not a drug-like intervention, lifespan intervention, or human result.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Review PMID 42230995 for possible somatic-mutation mechanism or animal mutation-burden support.

evidence_categories.rationale: The public baseline captures SIRT6 LINE1 repression, senescence LINE1 cDNA/type I interferon activation, and NRTI/lamivudine animal evidence. Coverage remains thin for the broader field because recent transposable-element review context, DREAM/somatic-mutation biology, TAF/FTC pharmacology boundaries, and downstream cGAS-STING/NLRP3 intervention context are not yet curated.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Review PMID 42219647, PMID 42230995, PMID 41929344, and selected cGAS-STING/NLRP3 sources only where they clarify the transposon-control boundary.

evidence_categories.rationale: The outlook names the key limitations around ART-AD size, open-label design, Alzheimer's disease context, downstream CANTOS/TET2 mechanism, and chronic preventive-use safety. Explicit limiting or trial-watch records for TAF/FTC biological-age evidence, LINE-AD, SANTA, TPN-101 healthy-aging activity, and TET2 canakinumab trial-watch evidence are not yet represented.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Coverage repair should decide whether missing items become public support, activity, limiting records, or explicit exclusions.

evidence_categories.rationale: ART-AD provides short-duration 3TC tolerability context in 12 early Alzheimer disease participants. TPN-101 and FTC/TAF sources are not yet curated, and no source establishes durable preventive-use safety, target engagement, resistance-context boundaries, tissue specificity, functional outcomes, or long-term healthspan benefit.

ART-AD NCT04552795 is traceable through the published source. Missing trial-watch context includes LINE-AD NCT04500847, SANTA NCT07210528, TET2 CHIP canakinumab NCT06691217, and adjacent inflammatory cardiovascular programs such as ZEUS NCT05021835 and POSIBIL6ESKD NCT05485961. The ARPA-H TPN-101 healthy-aging program was found through primary web sources but no ClinicalTrials.gov registry record was found during this pass.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Stage or explicitly exclude NCT04500847, NCT07210528, NCT06691217, NCT05021835, NCT05485961, and ARPA-H/TPN-101 program sources during coverage repair.

evidence_categories.rationale: The bootstrap used targeted primary and registry sources. Surveillance surfaced PMID 42219647, a recent transposable-elements-in-aging review chapter, but no formal recent-review or citation-network audit has been recorded for this track.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Run selective recent-review triangulation around LINE1/retrotransposons, NRTIs, cGAS-STING, SIRT6, DREAM, somatic mutation accumulation, and CHIP-directed inflammation.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline includes key LINE1/SIRT6, LINE1/senescence, 3TC mouse, ART-AD, CANTOS/TET2, and TIMI CHIP anchors. The newly surfaced FTC/TAF, TPN-101, DREAM, and LINE-AD/SANTA records show that the translational anchor set has not been systematically checked.

The public outlook correctly keeps LINE1/reverse-transcriptase biology separate from CHIP/TET2 inflammatory outcome evidence. Repair is still needed to prevent overgeneralizing among ART-AD 3TC, FTC/TAF biological-age signals, TPN-101 healthy-aging development, downstream cGAS-STING/NLRP3 biology, DREAM/DNA repair mutation-control biology, and cytokine modulation in CHIP.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Keep future public changes explicit about whether evidence supports direct LINE1 suppression, reverse-transcriptase-inhibitor pharmacology, somatic-mutation-burden control, CHIP clone dynamics, or downstream inflammatory-risk modulation.

search_log_summary: The surveillance pass reviewed the published outlook, bootstrap session, candidate bundle, evidence reviews, publication event, PubMed date-window and correction searches, ART-AD/LINE-AD/SANTA/CHIP-related ClinicalTrials.gov records, full bioRxiv and medRxiv date-window screens, and web/primary-source follow-up for ART-AD, DREAM, CHIP/canakinumab, TPN-101, ARPA-H, and FTC/TAF biological-age evidence.
source_selection_notes: PMID 42230995 was not staged because it is a same-window mechanistic and mouse somatic-mutation source, not a controlled human intervention or drug-like target-engagement result; it should be source-reviewed during coverage repair.

PMID 41929344 was not staged because it predates the June 2 baseline and is a medRxiv source that requires review for study design, sponsor/conflict boundaries, lack of direct transposable-element readouts, and DNA-methylation endpoint interpretation.
NCT04500847 and NCT07210528 were not staged because they are recruiting no-results Alzheimer NRTI registries without same-window result updates.

ARPA-H award 4201, Brown/University of Rochester material, and the Transposon Therapeutics release were not staged because they are pre-baseline program/activity leads for TPN-101 rather than clinical result evidence.

NCT06691217 was not staged because it is a recruiting no-results canakinumab/TET2 CHIP vascular-inflammation registry; it is important watchlist context but not current efficacy or clone-dynamics evidence.

Same-window PubMed and registry hits around cGAS-STING, NLRP3, CKD CHIP risk, MSA brain CH-gene mutations, STING inhibitors, and inflammatory cardiovascular trials were recorded as context only or wrong-scope where they lacked direct transposon or somatic-mutation control evidence.

known_gaps.description: PMID 41929344, NCT04500847, NCT07210528, ARPA-H award 4201, and TPN-101 program sources describe reverse-transcriptase-inhibitor biological-age or aging/Alzheimer trial context that is not represented in the current public support map or explicit exclusions.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run coverage repair to source-review the FTC/TAF biological-age preprint, LINE-AD, SANTA, and TPN-101/ARPA-H healthy-aging program before deciding whether to stage support, activity, limiting, or exclusion records.

known_gaps.description: PMID 42230995 reports DREAM-associated activity links to somatic mutation burden, mammalian lifespan, Alzheimer disease pathology, and mouse brain mutation accumulation, but it is not represented in the current support map.
known_gaps.suggested_action: During coverage repair, decide whether DREAM belongs as a public mechanism/animal mutation-burden support record or remains contextual because no drug-like human intervention is available.

known_gaps.description: The public outlook includes CANTOS/TET2 and TIMI CHIP records but does not include explicit trial-watch context for TET2 CHIP canakinumab NCT06691217 or adjacent inflammatory cardiovascular programs.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Review NCT06691217, NCT05021835, and NCT05485961 during repair, and keep direct clone-control evidence separate from downstream cytokine-risk modulation.

known_gaps.description: No curated or newly found human source shows direct reduction of somatic mutation burden, CHIP clone size, LINE1 cDNA, retrotransposon activity, or tissue-specific target engagement tied to functional aging or disease outcomes.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep the public stage at low-confidence human_biomarker_signal unless controlled human studies provide target-engagement and functional or disease-outcome evidence.

known_gaps.description: No formal recent-review or citation-network audit is recorded after the initial bootstrap, and surveillance surfaced a relevant 2026 transposable-elements-in-aging review.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Use PMID 42219647 and citation chasing to check for missing primary anchors around LINE1, NRTIs, cGAS-STING, DREAM, and CHIP before returning to ordinary surveillance.

known_gaps.description: The track combines LINE1/reverse-transcriptase biology, somatic mutation burden, CHIP inflammatory disease risk, DREAM/DNA repair biology, and downstream cytokine modulation, so one branch could easily be overgeneralized to another.
known_gaps.suggested_action: During repair, make branch-specific decisions about support roles and limitations so public wording does not imply that cytokine blockade, NRTIs, DREAM modulation, or CHIP outcomes are interchangeable.

next_coverage_action: Run a bounded coverage-repair pass focused on reverse-transcriptase-inhibitor human biomarker and trial-watch context, TPN-101/ARPA-H healthy-aging activity, DREAM/somatic-mutation biology, TET2 CHIP canakinumab trial-watch records, and recent review triangulation before ordinary surveillance resumes.
